algorithms.title: Pediatric Altered Mental Status Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for evaluating and managing a child with altered mental status.
steps: Note Appearance abnormalities on the PAT and confirm level of consciousness using AVPU.
Check blood glucose when a meter is available; treat hypoglycemia per protocol if present.

Work through AEIOU-TIPS to consider reversible causes: Alcohol/substance, Epilepsy/postictal, Infection, Overdose, Uremia/metabolic, Trauma/temperature, Insulin/hypoglycemia, Psychiatric, Shock/stroke.
Assess for signs of increased intracranial pressure: bulging fontanelle, decreasing consciousness, irregular respirations, Cushing triad.
Support airway, breathing, and circulation as needed; avoid hypoxia and hypotension.
Reassess mental status serially and treat a worsening trend as more concerning than any single value.

algorithms.title: Pediatric Seizure Field Management
algorithms.description: Sequence for managing an actively seizing child and recognizing status epilepticus.
steps: Protect the airway with positioning and suction as needed; do not place anything in the mouth.
Protect the child from injury during the seizure.
If the seizure resolves in under 5 minutes and the child returns to baseline, assess for simple vs complex febrile seizure criteria if fever is present, and transport for evaluation.

If the seizure lasts 5 minutes or longer, or seizures repeat without return to baseline, treat as status epilepticus — a time-critical emergency requiring immediate escalation (refer to PALS reviewer for benzodiazepine dosing).
Reassess airway, breathing, and mental status continuously during and after the seizure.
